# Copilot Instructions

## Build and Test

This repo uses the `devcontainer` CLI (`@devcontainers/cli`) for testing. It is preinstalled in the dev container.

```bash
# Run all tests (autogenerated + scenarios) for all features
make test

# Run tests for a single feature
devcontainer features test --features <feature-name> --base-image mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/base:ubuntu

# Run a single scenario for a feature
devcontainer features test --features <feature-name> --filter <scenario-name> --base-image mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/base:ubuntu

# Sync feature source into the test-project for interactive testing, then build it
make test-project-sync
make test-project-build
```

## Architecture

This is a [dev container features](https://containers.dev/features) repository. Each feature in `src/` is an independent, installable unit that extends a dev container.

### Feature layout

Every feature under `src/<feature-name>/` follows this structure:

- `devcontainer-feature.json` — Feature metadata: id, name, version, options, mounts, lifecycle commands, and install ordering (`installsAfter`).
- `install.sh` — Runs at container build time. Sets up symlinks, writes config, and copies lifecycle scripts into place.
- `oncreate.sh` / `poststart.sh` — Optional lifecycle scripts invoked via `onCreateCommand` or `postStartCommand` as declared in the feature metadata.
- `README.md` — **Auto-generated** by `devcontainers/action@v1`. Do not edit manually; update `NOTES.md` for changelog entries instead.
- `NOTES.md` — Changelog and supplementary notes appended to the generated README.

### Persistence pattern

Several features (azure-cli-persistence, azd-cli-persistence, shell-history) share a common persistence strategy:

1. Declare a Docker volume mount at `/dc/<name>` in `devcontainer-feature.json`.
2. In `install.sh`, move the user's existing config dir (e.g. `~/.azure`) to a backup, then symlink it to the mounted volume.
3. Use an `oncreate.sh` lifecycle script to handle migrations — merging extensions/data from the fresh image into the persisted volume, tracking what was copied via marker files under `/usr/local/share/stuartleeks-devcontainer-features/<feature>/markers/`.

### Tests

Tests live under `test/<feature-name>/` and use the `dev-container-features-test-lib` harness:

- `scenarios.json` — Defines test scenarios with base images, feature options, and remote user overrides.
- `test.sh` — Default test entry point; uses `check "<label>" "<command>"` assertions followed by `reportResults`.
- `<scenario-name>.sh` — Scenario-specific test script, typically a thin wrapper that sources a shared `_default.sh`.
- `_default.sh` — Shared test logic reused across scenarios for the same feature.

## Conventions

- Scripts use `set -e` and `#!/bin/sh` or `#!/usr/bin/env bash` shebangs.
- Feature options are accessed in `install.sh` as uppercase environment variables (e.g., option `host_name` becomes `$HOST_NAME`).
- The `_REMOTE_USER` and `_REMOTE_USER_HOME` variables are provided by the dev container runtime and used in install/lifecycle scripts for user-specific setup.
- READMEs are auto-generated — place human-written content in `NOTES.md`.
- Releases are manual (`workflow_dispatch` on `main`); the release workflow publishes features and opens a PR with updated docs.
